question_answered: Do raw reads support adenovirus signal?
goat_result: Reads mapped to adenovirus reference panel with strongest support for AdV-12-like genome
dog_result: Reads mapped to adenovirus reference panel with strongest support for AdV-18-like genome
interpretation: Raw sequencing reads support adenovirus presence in both samples
Support is distributed across most of the viral genome

question_answered: Can a sample-specific genome be reconstructed?
goat_result: Consensus genome reconstructed relative to X73487.1
dog_result: Consensus genome reconstructed relative to GU191019.1
interpretation: Recovered genomes represent sample-specific adenovirus sequences

question_answered: What are the closest known relatives?
goat_result: Closest hits to AdV-12 / Human mastadenovirus A genomes including African context
dog_result: Closest hits to AdV-18 / Human mastadenovirus A genomes including Tanzanian sequences
interpretation: Recovered genomes are consistent with AdV-12-like and AdV-18-like assignments
Both genomes fall within Human mastadenovirus A diversity
Recovered genomes are divergent from but closely related to references

goat_result: Variation enriched in hexon, fiber, penton, E2A and polymerase-related genes
dog_result: Variation observed in hexon, fiber, penton, protein IIIa and replication-related genes
interpretation: Coding variation supports comparative genomic differentiation

question_answered: Can adenovirus signal be recovered without reference-guided mapping?
goat_result: Multiple AdV-12-like contigs recovered, largest ~9.8 kb
dog_result: Multiple AdV-18-like contigs recovered, largest ~11.7 kb
interpretation: Independent assembly supports reference-guided genome recovery
